description: Demo of role-driven enemy tactics (Tactical Crucible #35). The four foes fight differently, so positioning and target choice matter: the SKELETON archer (artillery) hangs back, snipes the weakest of you, and KITES away when you close on it; the BUGBEAR (brute) charges and focuses whoever's most wounded to finish them; the GOBLIN (skirmisher) holds its bow's range and harries; and the BANDIT CAPTAIN (leader) anchors the squad's morale — the others won't break while it stands, but cut the captain down and watch their nerve collapse (the wounded flee or surrender). Verify: drop low — the brute comes for you; rush the skeleton — it backs off and keeps shooting; kill the captain — morale breaks.
customIntroduction: Four very different threats hold the waystation: a bandit captain barking orders from the middle of the yard, a skeleton archer perched at the back with an arrow already nocked, a hulking bugbear cracking its knuckles, and a goblin darting along the flank with a short bow.
customContext:
DEMO — ENEMY ROLES (#35). This is a COMBAT scene showcasing role-driven enemy AI (the engine resolves it — narrate what the Event Log shows; don't override their choices).

The four foes each have a tactical ROLE that changes how they position and pick targets:
1. SKELETON = ARTILLERY: stays at range, targets the WEAKEST/most-wounded party member (the squishy backline), and KITES backward if someone closes into melee — it won't stand and trade.
2. BUGBEAR = BRUTE: charges in and focuses the MOST-WOUNDED target to finish it — it ignores the nearest healthy foe to go for the kill.
3. GOBLIN WARRIOR = SKIRMISHER: holds its bow's range and harries (hit-and-run), composing with its Nimble Escape.
4. BANDIT CAPTAIN = LEADER: anchors the squad's MORALE. While the captain lives, the others hold the line — they won't flee or surrender. The moment the captain falls, morale collapses: wounded foes break (flee toward the map edge) or yield (#34 morale).

NARRATE the tactics: the captain's barked commands, the skeleton's cold retreat, the bugbear single-mindedly going for the bleeding, and — when the captain dies — the rout. Don't fake outcomes; the engine drives the positions, targets, flight, and surrender. This composes with reinforcements (#33) and morale (#34).
objective: Beat four foes that each fight differently. Watch the skeleton kite and snipe the weak, the bugbear hunt the wounded, and the captain hold the line — then drop the captain and break their morale.

then.message: DEMO (#35): four roles. Skeleton (artillery) snipes the weakest & kites; bugbear (brute) hunts the most-wounded; goblin (skirmisher) harries at range; bandit captain (leader) anchors morale. Drop the captain to break the squad.
